title: RKLB Q1 Deep Dive: Space Systems Expansion, Neutron Progress, and Record Backlog Fuel Outlook
text: Aerospace and defense company Rocket Lab announced better-than-expected revenue in Q1 2026, with sales up 63.5% year on year to $200.3 million. Next quarter's revenue guidance of $232.5 million at midpoint exceeded analyst expectations by 12%. The company posted a non-GAAP loss of $0.02 per share, beating estimates by $0.02. Q1 2026 highlights include revenue of $200.3 million vs $190.9 million estimate (4.9% beat), adjusted EPS of -$0.02 vs -$0.04 estimate, adjusted EBITDA of -$11.75 million (-5.9% margin), and Q2 guidance of $232.5 million revenue midpoint and $23 million EBITDA midpoint. Market cap reached $45.42 billion. Management attributed growth to robust demand for Electron and HASTE launches, record launch contract signings, and expansion in satellite platforms and subsystems. CEO Peter Beck emphasized record backlog, record cash position and record launch contracts, reflecting momentum across defense, commercial, and civil space sectors. Major catalysts included completing significant contracts, introducing proprietary technologies, and acquiring Motive Space Systems. The company signed record Electron and HASTE contracts, including a $190 million 20-launch HASTE agreement and five Neutron contracts, increasing backlog 20% quarterly. Space Systems revenue benefited from scaling satellite platforms for national security and commercial constellation projects.
